description: Fix incorrect 'restricted-access' claim in docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx. The doc says all @opentabs-dev/* platform packages are published with restricted access and that only authenticated npm users with access to the scope can install them. This is false — every platform package.json has `publishConfig.access: public`, and the packages are installable by anyone via `npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli`. The root CLAUDE.md correctly calls them 'public packages'. This finding is a real docs bug because it misleads users into thinking they need npm auth to install OpenTabs.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Correct publishing.mdx to describe packages as public, not restricted
userStories.description: Rewrite the two paragraphs in docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx that describe platform packages as restricted-access. The packages are public (access: public in every package.json publishConfig). Also fix the specific incorrect claim that 'npm defaults to restricted access for scoped packages' — every platform package overrides this with explicit `"access": "public"`.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx no longer contains the string 'restricted-access packages'
docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx no longer contains the claim that 'only authenticated npm users with access to the @opentabs-dev scope can install them'
docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx explicitly describes the packages as publicly published with `"publishConfig": { "access": "public" }` — or equivalent wording that matches reality
The intro paragraph (previously describing restricted-access) now says something like 'I publish the platform packages as public packages to the npm registry under the @opentabs-dev org.'
The `publishConfig` paragraph (previously lines ~104) accurately describes that all seven packages use `"publishConfig": { "access": "public" }` and are installable by anyone via npm
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx (correct two paragraphs + bump lastUpdated)

Context:
The current publishing doc is wrong on two places:

1. Line 7 (intro, under ## Packages heading):
> I publish the platform packages as restricted-access packages to the npm registry under the `@opentabs-dev` org.

2. Line 104 (right after the dependency-order table, around line 100-105):
> Six packages use `"publishConfig": { "access": "restricted" }` explicitly. `browser-extension` omits `publishConfig` but is still restricted — npm defaults to restricted access for scoped packages. Only authenticated npm users with access to the `@opentabs-dev` scope can install them.

Ground truth (verified by running `grep -A1 publishConfig platform/*/package.json`):

- `@opentabs-dev/shared`: `"access": "public"`
- `@opentabs-dev/browser-extension`: `"access": "public"`
- `@opentabs-dev/mcp-server`: `"access": "public"`
- `@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk`: `"access": "public"`
- `@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools`: `"access": "public"`
- `@opentabs-dev/cli`: `"access": "public"`
- `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin`: `"access": "public"`

All seven packages override `publishConfig.access` to `public`, and are installable by anyone. The root CLAUDE.md (platform/cli section) already says they are 'published as public packages to the npm registry under the @opentabs-dev org'.

Changes:
1. Replace line 7 (the intro): change 'restricted-access packages' to 'public packages'. Keep the tone (the author's first-person voice) consistent with the rest of the doc. A correct phrasing: 'I publish the platform packages as public packages to the npm registry under the `@opentabs-dev` org.'
2. Rewrite the paragraph around line 104 to be accurate. A correct phrasing: 'All seven packages use `"publishConfig": { "access": "public" }` — they are installable by anyone via `npm install -g <package>`. Scoped npm packages default to restricted access, so the explicit `public` override is required.'
3. Update the frontmatter `lastUpdated: 2026-04-15` at the top of the file to `lastUpdated: 2026-04-24`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the table of 7 packages with their dependency order — it is still accurate.
- Do NOT change any other text in the file. The rest of the publishing workflow (GitHub Actions, npm auth, version bumping, after-publish plugin rebuilds, etc.) is all correct.
- Do NOT touch any platform/*/package.json — those are already correct.
- Do NOT add or remove any headings or sections — only the two paragraphs in question change.
- The word 'public' must replace 'restricted-access' in a way that the voice still reads naturally. Keep the single-author first-person tone ('I publish ...').
- Before editing, grep for `restricted` in the file to ensure you catch every occurrence.
